domain: fermentation microbiology (kombucha SCOBY layered fermentation succession)
specific_mechanism: Kombucha SCOBY is a symbiotic culture of Acetobacter/Gluconobacter/Komagataeibacter (AAB), Lactobacillus (LAB), and yeasts (Brettanomyces, Saccharomyces, Zygosaccharomyces, Pichia). Fermentation proceeds in succession: yeasts dominate Day 0-4 converting sugars to ethanol+CO₂, then AAB convert ethanol+sugar to acetic acid, lowering pH. Each new batch grows a 'baby SCOBY' layer atop the 'mother SCOBY' — the layered structure is a written record of the succession. Embedded microbes share the interphase pellicle (oxygen+nutrients).
llm_application: Multi-agent LLM team as SCOBY: agents adopt stratified roles in temporal succession — early-batch 'yeast' agents do raw idea generation (high entropy, high CO₂-output), then 'AAB' agents convert proposals into structured outputs (low pH = sharpened claims), with a shared pellicle (working memory / scratchpad) buffering both. Each cycle deposits a 'baby layer' (a checkpoint of which roles fired in what order). Information cascade: the layered structure makes the team's emergent organization legible to a downstream auditor — you can read the SCOBY stack to reconstruct which agent specialized when.
novelty_claim: AgentNet (2504.00587) decentralized evolutionary coordination, AgentVerse role specialization, AgentNet dynamic specialization, 'Drop the Hierarchy and Roles' (2603.28990), and 'Emergent Coordination in Multi-Agent LMs' (2510.05174) all study self-organizing role specialization. The endogeneity paradox shows minimal scaffolding + endogenous specialization is optimal. None specifically frames the time-ordered output of a multi-agent run as a *stacked stratigraphic record* (baby-on-mother SCOBY layers) that an auditor can read to recover role-emergence history. Open question whether this auditing-stratigraphy framing is substantively new.
